Why CSS overflow: scroll Shows No Scrollbar on macOS Chrome (vs. Windows)

You set overflow: scroll, then realize Mac Chrome shows no scrollbar — but your coworker on Windows sees one fine. This post starts with the four overflow values, then explains macOS's Overlay Scrollbar mechanism — why Chrome on macOS doesn't always show a scrollbar even with overflow: scroll. Three fixes: ::-webkit-scrollbar to force it, the newer standard scrollbar-width and scrollbar-color, and flipping macOS's "Show scroll bars" system setting to "Always".
Chrome Keeps Caching Your Changes? Three Ways to Clear It Fast

You updated the page but Chrome keeps showing the old version. This post walks through three ways to clear Chrome's cache — keyboard shortcut, devtools, and an extension — plus a tip for how developers can avoid the problem in the first place.
How to Change Chrome DevTools' Language

Chrome DevTools has always defaulted to English, but starting around Chrome 94 it added language localization. This post is the quick how-to — opening DevTools settings, switching to your preferred language under Preferences, and reloading DevTools to apply.
Using Firefox for Frontend Dev: Features and Advantages

People keep asking what browser I use and why it isn't Chrome. So here's a quick post on Firefox's frontend dev features — better-than-Chrome Flex tooling, CSS Grid inspection, event listener inspection per element, accessibility tools like color blindness simulation, in-browser font tools, and an AJAX inspector that lets you edit and resend requests directly.
Core Web Vitals for SEO: LCP, FID, and CLS

Heads-up that this post is from 2021 — Google replaced FID with INP in March 2024. The general framing still applies. Originally written when I noticed my Google Search Console flagging my site with bad "page experience" scores — turns out Google's Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) had been quietly added in 2020 and started factoring into SEO. This post is a quick tour of the three metrics, their thresholds, and how to test your site.
Browser Forces Localhost to HTTPS? How to Clear HSTS in Chrome / Edge / Firefox

Web devs spend a lot of time on localhost and 127.0.0.1, but sometimes the browser forces HTTPS or throws "This site can't provide a secure connection" and "localhost refused to connect" errors. This post explains why — HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) — and how to clear it in Chrome, Edge, and Firefox.
